description_long: This object is a rectangular electronic device with a metallic, slightly weathered exterior and a flat top surface. The front panel features several rotary knobs, toggle switches, and input/output ports, as well as a display area, suggesting it is a piece of test or measurement equipment, such as a signal generator or frequency counter. The device has a foldable metal stand attached to the front, allowing it to be propped up at an angle for easier access to the controls. The rear panel includes a power socket, a fuse holder, and a power switch, indicating it is powered by an external AC source. The sides and top are plain, with a muted blue-gray finish and some visible wear, giving it an industrial, utilitarian appearance. The overall construction appears robust, suitable for use in workshops, garages, or technical environments.
description: A rectangular, metallic electronic device with control knobs, switches, and a display on the front, and a power socket on the back. It has a blue-gray weathered finish and a foldable metal stand.
description_view_0: The left side and top of the device are visible, showing the flat, blue-gray metallic surface and the foldable metal stand extending from the front.
description_view_1: The front panel is visible, featuring multiple rotary knobs, switches, ports, and a display area, along with the metal stand propping up the device.
description_view_2: The right side and top are visible, with the plain blue-gray surface and no visible controls or ports.
description_view_3: The rear panel is shown, displaying a power socket, fuse holder, and power switch, with the rest of the surface being plain and metallic.
attribute_control_interface: physical buttons, rotary dial, toggle switch
